hyperedges.label: Adaptive Selection Architecture Gap
hyperedges.description: The exercise selector implements adaptive rules A-E for selecting exercises, but PracticeQuestion bypasses them with random pickExercise(). This gap was identified as L2 and later resolved by consolidation removing pickExercise() in favor of session_runner-governed selection.

hyperedges.label: Mastery Computation Unification
hyperedges.description: Three separate mastery computation implementations existed (practice/page.tsx, session_runner.ts, exercise_selector.ts). L10 identified this as primitive. The canonical mastery model later unified them with weighted accuracy, recency, and session mode factors.

hyperedges.label: Reading Unit / TextGroup Evolution
hyperedges.description: The system evolved from L1 (exercise as atomic unit) through the proposed TextGroup/SessionContext abstractions to the implemented ReadingUnit type, reading-based session mode, and canonical text pack integration with multi-skill persistence.

hyperedges.label: TextGroup implementation path from limitation to execution
Agent pipeline evolution from documentary to operative
Reading unit ecosystem: candidates, packs, and engine
Weak Skill Strengthening System
hyperedges.description: The mastery model state machine, recommendation engine, and skill states together form the weak skill detection, prioritization, and reinforcement system.

hyperedges.label: Pedagogical Core Principles
hyperedges.description: Three core pedagogical principles that govern all content and exercise design: skill-first priority, immediate actionable feedback, and cognitive load management.

hyperedges.label: Reading Stimulus Design Decision
hyperedges.description: ADR-005 establishes that readings serve as internal stimuli subordinate to skill-first selection, with trainable_skills as the primary acceptance criterion overriding module_fit.
